title: Paneer Bhurji (Scrambled Paneer)

subs.name: 250 g firm tofu, crumbled — press out moisture first — dairy-free and equally high-protein
peas or corn kernels — add at the same stage
ghee for a richer flavour; adds a small amount of dairy fat

steps.text: Heat oil in a kadai on medium flame. Add onion and green chilli and sauté, stirring occasionally, until the onion turns soft and translucent, about 3 minutes. Do not let it brown or the masala will taste bitter.

Add ginger-garlic paste and fry for 1 minute until the raw smell is gone. Tip in the tomatoes and capsicum with a pinch of salt, then cook on medium-high for 4-5 minutes, pressing the tomatoes down with the back of the spoon, until they collapse and the oil begins to separate from the masala.
Reduce the flame to low. Add turmeric, red chilli powder and coriander powder, stir for 30 seconds to bloom the spices in the residual oil without letting them scorch.

Add the crumbled paneer and toss well to coat every piece in the masala. Increase flame to medium and cook for 3-4 minutes, stirring frequently, until the paneer picks up a light golden colour at the edges and is heated all the way through.
Sprinkle garam masala and finish with coriander leaves. Taste for salt and serve hot with whole-wheat rotis, toasted multigrain bread or directly alongside steamed rice for a high-protein meal.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.

substitution_notes: paneer → 250 g firm tofu, crumbled — press out moisture first — dairy-free and equally high-protein; oil → ghee for a richer flavour; adds a small amount of dairy fat; capsicum → peas or corn kernels — add at the same stage
